topic: CMB Axis of Evil — c6 queue verification: c5 H1 (stale detail_extra) + H2 (WIN-034 Unfalsifiable preemption) patches landed
change_summary: Queue item 348 (class:verification) asked us to verify (1) detail_extra closing paragraph rewritten to land Self-Contradicted verdict per c5 hole #1 major-fix, and (2) WIN-034 Unfalsifiable load-bearing-premise clarification inserted in detail_evidence per c5 hole #2 moderate-fix. Both patches landed verbatim per c5 recommendations (commit 5e798cb5, workspace-sync of decider 2026-05-14T01:22 run). detail_evidence length 3234 → 3732 (+498, matches expected insertion size). detail_extra closing paragraph stale 'Misleading still applies' line is GONE; current closing affirms 'the WIN is Self-Contradicted (escalated from Misleading 2026-05-09 per EXP-295)'. Verdict unchanged at Self-Contradicted. All other fingerprint-tracked fields (claim 16, finding 149, detail_verdict_text 1532, tldr_evidence 319, tldr_verdict 239) byte-identical to c5 baseline.
our_argument_summary: WIN-048 sits at verdict Self-Contradicted with the same SC anchor as c5: the WIN-034 copper-firmament dilemma with both defender branches (transparent-firmament contradicts WIN-034; thermal-source-at-2.725K produces featureless blackbody with no acoustic peaks/E-mode/lensing) closing on the dome's own commitments. The two patches the queue asked us to verify (c5 H1 + H2) close the c5-identified holes cleanly: the stale detail_extra 'Misleading still applies' line — the most quotable single tu quoque attack a defender could extract — is replaced with a sound Self-Contradicted closing; the WIN-034 Unfalsifiable-as-premise objection is now pre-rebutted inline in detail_evidence. c5's advocate-defense rating of 4 should now drop to 2 (the primary tu quoque attack is retired; only the more abstract WIN-034-Unfalsifiable-as-premise pivot remains, and that is now pre-rebutted).

dome_code_analysis.note: c6 is a queue-verification cycle for two prose patches. No code-tag re-validation required; neither patch turns on a code-tag claim.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Carried forward from c2/c3/c4/c5 — no monitor.py domain tracks CMB Axis of Evil; only static live-power dashboard entry. detail_verdict_text 1532 chars unchanged from c5; documents this verbatim.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Carried forward — no standard-physics 'Axis of Evil' explanation is being renamed; the dome adopts the anomaly's existence as confirmation without offering a different value.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Carried forward — Land & Magueijo 2005 (PRL 95, 071301) and Planck PR3/PR4 maps public well before dome monitor commit; detail_verdict_text still cites these.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Carried forward — no derivation chain from disc_radius/firmament_height/sun_altitude/moon_altitude to any CMB observable; detail_evidence still notes no analog in dome geometry.

patches_verified.expected_text_anchor: Net effect on verdict: the WIN is Self-Contradicted (escalated from Misleading 2026-05-09 per EXP-295)
patches_verified.verification_method: string match against current data/wins.json (post git pull --rebase to commit a4996e5b); both presence-of-new and absence-of-stale confirmed programmatically
patches_verified.patch_quality_notes: Patch lands the SC verdict cleanly and preserves the three-reasons structural narrative. Two minor stylistic flaws noted as new c6 holes (see holes_found): (a) phrase 'less misleading' in the patched paragraph is awkward now that the verdict label is Self-Contradicted rather than Misleading — the colloquial sense of 'misleading' is intended but a careful reader may conflate; (b) sentence 'Lowering the statistical significance does not change the opacity of copper' appears twice in the detail_extra block (once in list item 3, once in the new closing) — stylistic redundancy, harmless but visible.

patches_verified.expected_text_anchor: Our review classifies WIN-034 itself as Unfalsifiable on direct material-sampling grounds (we cannot sample the alleged dome material). That classification does not exempt a published dome commitment from load-bearing-premise status for SC pattern (2) reasoning
patches_verified.verification_method: string match against current data/wins.json; insertion placed correctly after the WIN-029 Schumann pre-rebuttal sentence (the 'firmament cannot be metaphorical for one purpose and physical for another' anchor), preserves flow
patches_verified.patch_quality_notes: Pre-rebuts the defender's strongest abstract objection (using-Unfalsifiable-as-premise) by drawing the explicit distinction between Unfalsifiable-on-material-sampling-grounds (the basis of WIN-034's classification) and load-bearing-premise-status-for-SC-pattern-(2)-reasoning (the basis of WIN-048's verdict). One minor jargon issue noted as new c6 hole: 'SC pattern (2)' appears without inline definition or anchor link; readers who haven't been through Part 5 (Kill Shots) may not know what it refers to.

holes_found.description: EXP-295 batch sub_rationale handling is internally inconsistent across the five-WIN MOND-grouping reclassification (WIN-047, 048, 052, 054, 055). Current state in data/wins.json (verified this cycle): WIN-047 sub_rationale was PATCHED 'See Part 6.13' → 'See Part 6.14' (per ISS-2002 closure 2026-05-14T01:24:13Z, resolution 'fixed', notes 'EXP-369 P1 manual apply'); WIN-048/052/054 sub_rationales all still read 'See Part 6.13' verbatim and were closed as wontfix-OBE (ISS-1922 closure 2026-05-14T01:24:13Z, same decider run as the WIN-047 patch, resolution_reason 'audit-trail-only, Part 6.13 reference is in unrendered field, harmless'); WIN-055 has no sub_rationale field at all. The decider has applied two mutually exclusive policies to the same identical dangling-reference pattern: 'fix it' for WIN-047, 'wontfix it as harmless' for WIN-048/052/054. If unrendered fields with dangling references are harmless, none of the patches were needed. If they're worth patching, all four should match WIN-047's treatment. The current heterogeneous state is the exact kind of cross-WIN inconsistency our review criticizes the dome model for. Additionally, even the 'patched' WIN-047 reference is semantically off-target: Part 6.14 is the κ Coupling Cluster section (verified in data/sections.json: p6-614 exists, p6-613 does not exist), which has nothing to do with MOND grouping (the topic of every sub_rationale in this batch); the patch points at a real section but a semantically irrelevant one. The honest fix here is structural: either (a) integrate Part 6.13 MOND-grouping prose as originally proposed in EXP-295 (the proposed_subsection_html draft was already approved by the analyst per c5 review), at which point all five WINs' sub_rationale references resolve correctly; OR (b) rewrite all five sub_rationale fields to reference monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-295-mond-grouping-verdict-review.json directly (the actual audit-trail target). The current state is neither.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should choose ONE of the two options above and apply it uniformly across all five WINs. Recommended option (a) if the analyst's Part 6.13 prose draft is still wanted — the proposed_subsection_html in EXP-295-mond-grouping-verdict-review.json is complete and approved. Recommended option (b) if Part 6.13 was abandoned for separate reasons — then revert the WIN-047 patch from 'Part 6.14' to the EXP file path, and apply the same EXP file path to WIN-048/052/054 in place of 'Part 6.13', and backfill the analogous sub_rationale on WIN-055. Either way: uniformity matters; the current state is auditable evidence that a batch was reviewed and only partially patched. Note: this is not a regression of the c5 hole #1 or c5 hole #2 patches — those landed cleanly and are independently verified above. This finding surfaces a separate cross-WIN inconsistency that c5 partially flagged in its 'also noted out-of-scope' paragraph but that was not made into a discrete actionable hole at the time. ISS-2002 (closed 'fixed') and ISS-1922 (closed 'wontfix') both went through the same decider run (decider-2026-05-14T01-22) — the run had access to both records simultaneously and still produced the asymmetric outcome.
holes_found.iss_link: Spans the resolved ISS-1922 (WIN-048 wontfix) + ISS-2002 (WIN-047 fixed) pair — needs a new ISS or amendment to one of the closed ones.

holes_found.description: Stylistic redundancy in the patched detail_extra closing paragraph. The sentence 'Lowering the statistical significance does not change the opacity of copper' appears twice in close proximity: once in list item 3 ('Lowering the statistical significance does not change the opacity of copper.') and once in the new closing paragraph ('Lowering the statistical significance does not change the opacity of copper or the absence of a dome cosmology, so the structural verdict stands independent of how the Planck reanalysis lands.'). The two occurrences are within ~200 characters of each other in the rendered output. Additionally, the new closing's phrase 'The weakened statistical claim is less misleading than the original' is awkward given the verdict label is now Self-Contradicted (no longer Misleading) — the colloquial-sense use of 'misleading' is correct but a careful reader may briefly conflate it with the verdict label that the same WIN just escalated past.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish patch, NOT blocking. Either: (a) remove the duplicate 'Lowering the statistical significance does not change the opacity of copper' from list item 3 (the closing paragraph already carries the point); OR (b) rephrase the new closing paragraph to 'The weakened statistical claim is less misleading than the original' → 'The dome author\'s self-correction is intellectually creditable' (same kernel-of-truth content, no verb collision with the prior verdict label). Either change costs ~50 chars. Neither change is necessary for verdict integrity.

holes_found.description: The c5 H2 patch introduces the phrase 'SC pattern (2) reasoning' into detail_evidence without inline definition, anchor link, or first-mention gloss. The 'SC pattern' framework is defined in Part 5 (Kill Shots) and elsewhere, but a reader who lands on WIN-048 panel directly (e.g., from an external link) may not know what 'SC pattern (2)' refers to. The reasoning move the sentence makes (Unfalsifiable on material-sampling vs load-bearing-premise) is sound but the load-bearing label is jargon-only.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional. Add a short anchor link or gloss on first mention: 'load-bearing-premise status for SC pattern (2) reasoning (see Part 5 SC patterns)' or '...for SC pattern (2) reasoning (i.e., where the dome\'s own commitments are turned against another of the dome\'s commitments)'. Not blocking; the inferential weight of the sentence still carries even for an uninformed reader.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: c5 hole #2 (the cheap interim WIN-034-Unfalsifiable-as-premise clarification) is now CLOSED by the queue_id 348 patch verified above. ISS-1921 itself (the broader WIN-034 reclassification proposal — c5 option b) remains open with status 'assigned-analyst' (verified in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json). Listed here for decider visibility: c6 does not re-raise option (a) because it's now landed; option (b) continues per ISS-1921 normal flow. No action needed from decider on this ISS in the c6 integration; this entry is informational continuity.

stronger_arguments.description: Carry-over from c5: WIN-034's own detail_evidence has an internal unit-frequency error in its skin-depth example. WIN-034 states 'copper's skin depth is ~0.066 mm at 1 GHz' (verified verbatim in current data/wins.json:WIN-034:detail_evidence). Computed values (also verified this cycle): skin depth δ = 1/√(πfμ₀σ) with σ_Cu = 5.96×10⁷ S/m gives δ = 0.163 μm at 160 GHz, 2.06 μm at 1 GHz, 65.2 μm = 0.0652 mm at 1 MHz. WIN-034's '~0.066 mm at 1 GHz' is the correct value for 1 MHz, off by a factor of √1000 ≈ 31.6 in frequency. The qualitative point (copper opaque to all radio) is unaffected; only the example number is wrong. This is a WIN-034 hole, not a WIN-048 hole, but it intersects with WIN-048's argument because both WINs invoke copper-skin-depth as load-bearing reasoning and the numbers should be self-consistent across the pair. WIN-048's '~0.2 μm at 160 GHz' (in detail_evidence and tldr_verdict) and '~0.163 μm' (implicit in the kernel_of_truth field) are both correct. c5 surfaced this in stronger_arguments and recommended either (a) a discrete WIN-034 fix issue, or (b) fold into ISS-1921's eventual EXP. As of c6, neither has happened — no discrete ISS exists for the WIN-034 frequency error (verified by grep of open-issues.json for 'skin depth', '1 GHz', '1 MHz', and 'WIN-034' — zero matches on the numeric fix). Recommend the decider open a WIN-034 issue OR amend ISS-1921's analyst-routing notes to include this fix in the eventual EXP scope. Cost is one line of analysis; the numeric fix is trivial.
sources: data/wins.json:WIN-034:detail_evidence — verbatim '~0.066 mm at 1 GHz' (frequency/depth swap; correct at 1 MHz)
data/wins.json:WIN-048:detail_evidence — verbatim '~0.2 μm at 160 GHz' (correct; computed 0.163 μm)
Quantitative skin-depth recomputation (this review's quantitative_verification field)
monitor/decisions/open-issues.json — no current ISS tracks this WIN-034 numeric fix

kernel_of_truth.description: The decider chose to apply the c5 hole #1 (major) and c5 hole #2 (moderate) patches as recommended in a single push (data/wins.json edits verified in commit 5e798cb5 at 2026-05-14T02:05:43Z, originating from decider run 2026-05-14T01-22). This is the cheap-fix-quickly behavior our review's process discipline asks for: identified holes patched in the same run, queue_id 348 pushed for verification, no extended back-and-forth. The patches themselves track the c5 review's text recommendations verbatim — the decider didn't second-guess the curmudgeon's suggested wording, which is the right move when c5's recommended text was already engineered to land the SC verdict cleanly (see c5 review file monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-048.c5.json hole #1 and hole #2 recommendation fields).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The cheap-fix-quickly process behavior is genuinely creditable and the verdict change Misleading → Self-Contradicted is now defensible on the dome's published surface. But the simultaneous handling of the EXP-295 batch sub_rationale references in the same decider run (decider-2026-05-14T01-22) was not uniform: WIN-047 sub_rationale was patched 6.13→6.14 (per ISS-2002 fix), while WIN-048/052/054 with identical patterns were closed as wontfix-OBE (per ISS-1922) and WIN-055 was left without the field entirely. The cheap-fix discipline that handled the c5 H1/H2 patches well did NOT carry through to uniform handling of the audit-trail field across the parallel batch. That's the c6 moderate finding above. (To be evenhanded: the operator-preference instruction asks me to consider the other side. A defender of the asymmetric decision might argue that WIN-047's patch was a targeted fix from a separate ISS chain — ISS-2002's notes cite 'EXP-369 P1 manual apply' rather than the same closure batch as ISS-1922 — so the decider may have followed two parallel work streams that happened to converge in the same closure timestamp. That's a fair charitable read; the operational counter is that the decider has both records in scope at closure time and could have either applied or reverted to uniformity — neither happened, so the asymmetric state is what's now committed.

next_action_notes: Both queue patches LANDED cleanly and close the c5 H1+H2 holes as expected. Decider should mark queue_id 348 popped. Three new c6 findings: (1) MODERATE batch sub_rationale inconsistency across EXP-295 5-WIN group — needs a new ISS or amendment to the closed ISS-1922 pair; (2) MINOR detail_extra stylistic redundancy in the just-patched closing paragraph — optional polish; (3) MINOR 'SC pattern (2)' jargon in the just-patched detail_evidence insertion — optional gloss/link. The WIN-034 1 GHz/1 MHz unit error is repeated in stronger_arguments as a carry-over carry-over — c5 raised it, c6 confirms still present, no ISS exists yet.
deeper_analysis: c6 is a queue-verification cycle (class:verification per PROP-025) for the two patches the decider applied per c5's hole #1 and #2 recommendations. Both patches landed verbatim. The verdict change Misleading → Self-Contradicted is now consistent across the rendered surfaces a reader sees: verdict badge says Self-Contradicted, tldr_verdict anchors on the copper-skin-depth opacity argument, detail_evidence pre-rebuts the WIN-034-Unfalsifiable-as-premise objection, detail_extra closing paragraph affirms 'the WIN is Self-Contradicted' explicitly. The c5 advocate-defense rating of 4 (primary tu quoque on stale detail_extra; secondary on WIN-034-as-Unfalsifiable-premise) should now drop to ~2 — the primary attack is retired by the H1 patch, and the secondary is pre-rebutted by the H2 patch. What remains: the cross-WIN consistency finding (EXP-295 batch sub_rationale heterogeneity) is a fresh c6 moderate that surfaced because the same decider run that applied H1/H2 also closed the parallel ISS-1922/ISS-2002 pair asymmetrically. This isn't a regression of either patch — it's a separate process finding that c5 partially flagged but didn't make actionable. The WIN-034 1 GHz/1 MHz skin-depth unit error remains uncaptured by any open ISS as of c6 — explicit stronger_argument note for decider to either open a discrete WIN-034 fix issue or amend ISS-1921's eventual EXP scope. Net: queue_id 348 is GREEN; verdict change is sound; three new c6 holes (1 moderate, 2 minor) flagged for downstream work.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Post-H1+H2-patches, the dome defender's strongest move shifts. The primary c5 attack (stale detail_extra → tu quoque) is retired — the detail_extra now explicitly lands Self-Contradicted. The secondary c5 attack (WIN-034-as-Unfalsifiable-premise) is pre-rebutted inline in detail_evidence. What's left: (1) the meta-process attack — 'your verdict was Misleading on May 9 morning, Self-Contradicted on May 9 afternoon per EXP-295, and your detail_extra was inconsistent with the verdict label for four more days until you patched it on May 14. Your audit-trail shows the same kind of label-vs-prose drift you criticize the dome for.' This is now a CLOSED bug, but a defender can still cite the timestamps to argue our verdict labels move faster than our prose can keep up. (2) The cross-WIN consistency attack lands cleanly on the EXP-295 batch sub_rationale heterogeneity: 'You patched WIN-047 sub_rationale 6.13→6.14 but closed WIN-048/052/054 with the same pattern as wontfix-as-harmless. Pick one. Your decider has been internally inconsistent within a single run.' (3) The 'SC pattern (2)' jargon in the new detail_evidence insertion may briefly confuse a defender-reader, but they won't weaponize confusion — they'll just shrug past it. Defense_survives drops from 4 (c5) to 2 (c6): attack (1) is closed-but-historically-quotable (low ammunition), attack (2) is live and quotable (medium ammunition), attack (3) is low-stakes cosmetic.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: The primary defenses from c5 (attacks 1 and 2 of the c5 list) are CLOSED by the H1+H2 patches. The fresh c6 attack (attack 2 above — cross-WIN sub_rationale inconsistency) is closed by either of the two uniform-handling options recommended in c6 hole #1 (integrate Part 6.13 prose OR rewrite all five sub_rationales to point at the EXP file). The historical-timestamp attack (c6 attack 1 above) is unfixable — you can't unwrite five days of stale detail_extra — but it's also low-grade ammunition that a serious dome defender wouldn't lead with.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: 1. EXP-295 batch sub_rationale handling is non-uniform across WIN-047/048/052/054/055 (the five-WIN MOND-grouping reclassification group). Verified state per current data/wins.json: WIN-047 references 'Part 6.14' (patched per ISS-2002 'fixed'); WIN-048/052/054 reference 'Part 6.13' (wontfix-OBE per ISS-1922); WIN-055 has no sub_rationale field. Part 6.14 exists in data/sections.json as the κ Coupling Cluster (semantically unrelated to MOND grouping); Part 6.13 does not exist. This is the c6 moderate finding (holes_found[0]). 2. Carry-over from c5: WIN-034 detail_evidence numeric example '~0.066 mm at 1 GHz' is incorrect (correct at 1 MHz). WIN-048 cites '~0.2 μm at 160 GHz' which is correct. The pair of skin-depth invocations should be numerically self-consistent. Routed to stronger_arguments for c6 (no discrete ISS for the WIN-034 fix as of this run). WIN-029 (Schumann claim) remains internally compatible with the patched WIN-048 prose — the WIN-029 Schumann chain pre-rebuttal in detail_evidence is unchanged and the new Unfalsifiable-clarification sits naturally after it.
cross_win_consistency.cross_win_finding_routed_to: (1) MODERATE c6 hole #1 — EXP-295 batch sub_rationale inconsistency; (2) stronger_arguments[0] — WIN-034 frequency/depth unit error, repeat carry-over from c5

Length deltas: detail_evidence c5→c6 +498 chars (expected ~496 from c5 H2 recommended insertion text — matches); detail_extra c5→c6 fully rewritten (c5 didn't track length; c6 records 3414 as baseline)
quantitative_verification.computations: Skin depth δ = 1/√(πfμ₀σ) with σ_Cu = 5.96×10⁷ S/m and μ = μ₀. f=160 GHz → δ = 0.163 μm; f=1 GHz → δ = 2.06 μm = 0.00206 mm; f=1 MHz → δ = 65.2 μm = 0.0652 mm. WIN-034's '~0.066 mm' value attributed to 1 GHz matches 1 MHz to within rounding (0.0652 vs 0.066 mm), off by frequency factor √1000 ≈ 31.6.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Same as c5: WIN-048 internal numerics VERIFIED; WIN-034 cross-cite has the 1 GHz / 1 MHz frequency-vs-depth swap. No new numerical claim introduced by the H1 or H2 patch; the H2 insertion contains no numbers, the H1 closing paragraph contains no numbers.

package_summary_for_decider: c6 queue verification of WIN-048 (queue_id 348, class:verification). VERDICT: GREEN on both patches — c5 H1 (stale detail_extra → Self-Contradicted closing) and c5 H2 (WIN-034 Unfalsifiable load-bearing-premise clarification in detail_evidence) both landed verbatim per c5 recommendations. The verdict change Misleading → Self-Contradicted (EXP-295, 2026-05-09) is now defensible on the dome's published surface; advocate-mode defense_survives drops from 4 (c5) to 2 (c6). Recommended action: no_change to WIN-048 specifically — decider should mark queue_id 348 popped on the strength of this review. THREE NEW c6 findings flagged separately, none blocking the verification: (1) MODERATE — EXP-295 batch sub_rationale handling is non-uniform across WIN-047/048/052/054/055 (WIN-047 patched 6.13→6.14, WIN-048/052/054 wontfix-OBE, WIN-055 has no field); needs a new ISS or amendment to the closed ISS-1922/ISS-2002 pair to restore uniformity. Recommended fix: integrate Part 6.13 MOND-grouping prose (EXP-295 proposed_subsection_html is approved and complete) OR rewrite all five sub_rationales to reference the EXP file directly. (2) MINOR — stylistic redundancy in the just-patched detail_extra closing paragraph (duplicate 'opacity of copper' sentence) + slightly awkward 'less misleading' phrasing given the verdict label is no longer Misleading. Optional polish. (3) MINOR — 'SC pattern (2)' jargon in the just-patched detail_evidence insertion lacks inline definition/anchor; optional gloss. CARRY-OVER from c5 (not new): WIN-034's '~0.066 mm at 1 GHz' skin-depth example has frequency/depth unit swap (correct at 1 MHz); no discrete ISS tracks this; recommend opening a WIN-034 fix issue OR folding into ISS-1921's eventual EXP scope.
